case_ch: In May 2026, while consolidating reimbursement materials for a Beijing trip related to the same project phase, the research team submitted the travel claims of Academician Zhao and the accompanying Prof. Lin together for review. Both charged their expenses to research project funds, both traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), both stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, and both submitted intercity transport invoices and lodging invoices. Both claimed city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts and claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300. Neither trip was made by the unit head personally; both submissions included a "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" along with materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal. Academician Zhao stayed under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night; Prof. Lin, a deputy senior-title and above professional-technical personnel, likewise submitted materials under the bureau-director accommodation standard, but the actual lodging rate written on the form was RMB 2,000 per night. Neither submission included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor any cases of transport or meal subsidies already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school reviewed the overseas travel expenses of two faculty members after their return in the same batch. Prof. Sun filed under non-research project funds as bureau-director-level personnel and above (management role); her overseas expense statement listed registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs. The boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all attached, and the materials had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center. The original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were both included; all overseas expenses were reimbursed together after her return, foreign currency had been selected in the appointment and converted automatically by the system, and there were no overseas book or material purchases, nor any prepayments for future travel. Prof. Gu, submitted in the same batch and corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel, had the same listed expense items and receipt preparation and had also been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; transport modes included economy-class airfare, hard-seat train, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin. However, only the overseas expense statement was attached in Prof. Gu's materials — no original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was found; there were likewise no overseas book or material purchases, nor any prepayments for future travel.

case_ch: In June 2026, the school held two consecutive themed training sessions, with all reimbursement materials organized together by the same administrator. The training session for bureau-director-level personnel lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,000, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 350, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,250. An out-of-town instructor was also engaged, incurring lecture fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 400, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300 for the instructor; written approval was attached for items exceeding the standard rate, but the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" was not included in this set of materials. The immediately following training session for division-level and lower personnel also lasted 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 1,700, meals of RMB 650, venue/materials/transport of RMB 250, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 2,750; the out-of-town instructor's fees remained at RMB 1,000 for lecture fees, RMB 340 for accommodation, RMB 130 for meals, and RMB 300 for transport. For this session, the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached.

case_ch: In September 2026, the school processed expert labor service fees together after an academic week event. Prof. Zhou gave a 4-class-hour thematic lecture and also participated in a 2-day in-person consultation; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis, calculated at RMB 450 per class hour under the division-level and lower personnel standard, and expert consulting fees at RMB 450 per day under the same personnel standard. Also arriving in the same batch was Prof. Wu, a national major research project leader, who likewise completed a 4-class-hour lecture and a 2-day in-person consultation; lecture fees were also declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 1,200 per class hour, with in-person consulting fees at RMB 1,000 per day. The entire batch of documents covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review and defense expenses included.

case_ch: In May 2026, the research team submitted the reimbursement materials for two colleagues' Beijing trips to advance a project, consolidated on a single list. Prof. Zheng, categorized as other personnel, charged expenses to research project funds, traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, filed accommodation under the bureau-director accommodation standard but wrote the actual price as RMB 2,000 per night, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and claimed city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts; the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal, intercity transport invoices, and lodging invoices were all attached. The accompanying Prof. Xu, a national major research project leader, likewise traveled by first-class train seating (round trip) for a 3-day, 2-night trip; accommodation was filed under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night, with a meal subsidy of RMB 300 and city transport expenses of RMB 180 claimed at actual cost based on receipts. However, the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" in Prof. Xu's submission was incomplete, and materials bearing the unit head's signature and department official seal were also missing. Neither submission included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor of transport or meal subsidies already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school reviewed the reimbursements for two scholars' overseas academic activities in the same batch. Prof. Jiang, classified as a national Level-2 professional-technical position personnel, filed expenses under non-research project funds; the overseas expense statement listed registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs. The boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all prepared and had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; all expenses were reimbursed together after returning to China, and foreign currency had been selected in the appointment and converted automatically by the system. However, no original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was found in the materials. Academician Shao, submitted in the same batch, had the same listed expense items; the boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all present and had also been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were both attached. Neither submission involved overseas book or material purchases, nor any prepayments for future travel.

case_ch: In July 2026, the school organized two consecutive high-level training sessions, with the administrator binding the two sets of materials together for reimbursement. The training session for academician-level participants lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,800; an out-of-town instructor was also engaged with lecture fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 500, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached, and written approval for items exceeding the standard rate was also included. The immediately following session for bureau-director-level personnel and above (management roles) also lasted 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880; the out-of-town instructor's fees remained at lecture fees of RMB 1,000, lodging of RMB 500, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300. The relevant "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," trainee list with contact information, itemized expense receipts from the training service provider, and written approval for items exceeding the standard rate were likewise all complete.

case_ch: In September 2026, the school processed the labor service fees of two experts together after a forum. The invited Prof. Han, corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel and above (management roles), gave a 4-class-hour lecture and participated in a 2-day in-person consultation; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 1,200 per class hour, with consulting fees at RMB 1,000 per day. The accompanying Prof. Lu, corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel, likewise completed a 4-class-hour lecture and a 2-day in-person consultation, but lecture fees were not declared on an after-tax basis; the lecture rate was RMB 900 per class hour and the in-person consulting rate was RMB 700 per day. The entire batch of documents covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review and defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the project team submitted the reimbursements for two faculty members' Beijing trips for a phase of project work together. Prof. Qian, bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank, traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, filed accommodation under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and claimed city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts; however, the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" in his materials was incomplete. The accompanying Prof. Shen, a professional-technical personnel at Grade 5 or above on the post-based salary scale, had the same itinerary, transport mode, accommodation standard, and subsidy basis; the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" and materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal were all fully attached. Both submitted intercity transport invoices and lodging invoices, and neither trip was made by the unit head personally. There were no approved special circumstances explanations or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, no approved special circumstances explanations or handwritten receipts signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained, and no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit or transport and meal subsidies being claimed twice.

case_ch: In 2026, the school reviewed the overseas expenses of two faculty members after their return in a consolidated batch. Prof. Cheng, corresponding to division-level and lower personnel, filed registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs under non-research project funds; the boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all present, and the materials had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were both attached. The other faculty member, Prof. Gao, a national major research project leader, had the same listed expense items and reimbursement approach, also reimbursing all overseas expenses in a single consolidated submission after returning; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were likewise complete. Both had selected foreign currency in their appointment booking with conversion handled automatically by the system; neither involved overseas book or material purchases or prepayments for future travel.

case_ch: In June 2026, the school held two consecutive training sessions as part of the same capacity-building initiative. The training session for national major research project leaders lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880; an out-of-town instructor's fees of RMB 1,000 for lecture fees plus RMB 500 for lodging, RMB 150 for meals, and RMB 300 for transport were also included. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached, and written approval for items exceeding the standard rate was included. The other session, for national Level-2 professional-technical position personnel, also lasted 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,800; the instructor fee structure was the same as the previous session, but the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" was not included in this set of materials. Written approval for items exceeding the standard rate was otherwise still attached.

case_ch: In September 2026, after an interdisciplinary forum, the school processed the labor service fees of two experts in the same batch. Prof. Yan, a national Level-2 professional-technical position personnel, gave a 4-class-hour lecture and also participated in a 2-day in-person consultation; lecture fees were not declared on an after-tax basis, with the lecture rate at RMB 1,200 per class hour and the in-person consulting rate at RMB 700 per day. Academician Fu, arriving in the same batch, likewise completed a 4-class-hour lecture and a 2-day in-person consultation; the lecture rate remained at RMB 1,200 per class hour, but lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis, and consulting fees were at RMB 700 per day. The personnel standard applied to both experts remained consistent across the lecture and consulting components; the entire batch of materials also contained no graduate thesis review and defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, while once again consolidating travel materials for a phase of work in Beijing, the research team included Academician Xie and the accompanying Prof. Fang in the same batch. Both charged expenses to research project funds, both traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), both stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, both claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300 and city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal, intercity transport invoices, and lodging invoices were all attached; neither trip was made by the unit head personally. Academician Xie stayed under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night; Prof. Fang, a deputy senior-title and above professional-technical personnel, likewise filed materials under the bureau-director accommodation standard, but the actual lodging rate was written as RMB 2,000 per night. Neither submission included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor any cases of transport or meal expenses already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school reviewed the overseas travel expenses of two faculty members in administrative roles in the same reimbursement review. Prof. Qiu, corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel and above (management roles), filed registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs under non-research project funds; the boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were complete and had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were both attached. The other faculty member, Prof. Luo, corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel, had the same listed expense items, receipt preparation, and arrangement of reimbursing all expenses together after return; foreign currency had also been selected in the appointment and converted automatically by the system. However, no original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was found in Prof. Luo's materials. Neither submission involved overseas book or material purchases, nor any prepayments for future travel.

case_ch: In June 2026, two groups of trainees were arranged in sequence within the same round of themed training, with the administrator submitting both sets of materials together for review. The training for bureau-director-level personnel lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,000, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 350, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,250; an out-of-town instructor's fees of RMB 1,000 for lecture fees plus RMB 400 for accommodation, RMB 150 for meals, and RMB 300 for transport were also included; written approval was attached for items exceeding the standard rate, but the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" was not included. The subsequent training for division-level and lower personnel also lasted 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 1,700, meals of RMB 650, venue/materials/transport of RMB 250, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 2,750; the instructor's lecture fees of RMB 1,000, accommodation of RMB 340, meals of RMB 130, and transport of RMB 300 were likewise included; the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached.

case_ch: In September 2026, the school processed the labor service fees of two experts together after a series of academic exchange events. Prof. He, the first to arrive, completed a 4-class-hour lecture and participated in a 2-day in-person consultation under the division-level and lower personnel standard; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 450 per class hour, and consulting fees at RMB 450 per day. Prof. Pan, a national major research project leader who arrived subsequently, also gave a 4-class-hour lecture and participated in a 2-day in-person consultation; lecture fees were likewise declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 1,200 per class hour, with consulting fees at RMB 1,000 per day. The entire batch of documents covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no doctoral or master's thesis review and defense expenses.

Prof. Tang, categorized as other personnel, charged expenses to research project funds, traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, filed accommodation under the bureau-director accommodation standard but wrote the actual price as RMB 2,000 per night, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and claimed city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts; the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal, intercity transport invoices, and lodging invoices were all attached. Neither submission included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor of transport or meal subsidies already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school reviewed the overseas conference expenses of two scholars after their return in the same batch. Prof. Gu, corresponding to a national Level-2 professional-technical position personnel, filed registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs under non-research project funds; the boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all attached and had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center. All overseas expenses were reimbursed together after her return, and foreign currency had been selected in the appointment and converted automatically by the system; transport modes included economy-class airfare, hard-seat rail, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin. However, no original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was found in her materials. Accompanying Academician Dong had the same listed expense items, the same transport modes, receipts, and review signatures; both the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were attached. Neither submission involved overseas book or material purchases, nor any prepayment for future travel.

case_ch: In July 2026, the school compiled and submitted the expenses for two high-level training sessions in the same reimbursement batch. The training session for academician-level participants lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,800; an out-of-town instructor's fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 500, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300 were also included. The other session, for bureau-director-level personnel and above (management roles), also lasted 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880; the instructor fee structure remained the same. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached for both sessions, and written approval for items exceeding the standard rate was included for all relevant items.

case_ch: In 2026, the school reviewed the expenses of two faculty members after their overseas academic activities in the same batch. Prof. Ren, corresponding to division-level and lower personnel, filed registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs under non-research project funds; the boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all present and had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were both complete; transport modes included economy-class airfare, hard-seat rail, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin. Prof. Deng, a national major research project leader submitted in the same batch, had the same listed expense items and the same arrangement of reimbursing all expenses together after return; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were likewise attached. Both had selected foreign currency in their appointment booking with conversion handled automatically by the system; neither submission involved overseas book or material purchases, nor any prepayment for future travel.

case_ch: In June 2026, the school held two consecutive high-level training sessions, with the reimbursement materials organized together by the same administrator. The training session for national major research project leaders lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880; an out-of-town instructor's fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 500, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300 were also included; the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached, and written approval for items exceeding the standard rate was included. The immediately following session, for national Level-2 professional-technical position personnel, also lasted 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,800; the instructor fee structure remained the same; however, the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" was not included in this set of materials.

case_ch: In September 2026, the school settled the labor service fees for two high-level experts following an exchange week event in the same batch. Prof. Su, a national Level-2 professional-technical position personnel, completed a 4-class-hour lecture and participated in a 2-day in-person consultation; lecture fees were not declared on an after-tax basis, with the lecture rate at RMB 1,200 per class hour and the in-person consulting rate at RMB 700 per day. Academician He, arriving in the same batch, likewise completed a 4-class-hour lecture and a 2-day in-person consultation; the lecture rate remained at RMB 1,200 per class hour and the consulting rate at RMB 700 per day, but lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis. The entire batch of documents covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review and defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the research team once again submitted the travel materials for two colleagues' Beijing trips to advance the project for review in the same batch. Academician Lu and the accompanying Prof. Peng both charged expenses to research project funds, both traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), both stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, both claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300 and city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal, intercity transport invoices, and lodging invoices were all attached; neither trip was made by the unit head personally. Academician Lu stayed under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night; Prof. Peng, a deputy senior-title and above professional-technical personnel, likewise filed materials under the bureau-director accommodation standard, but the actual lodging rate was written as RMB 2,000 per night. Neither submission included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor any cases of transport or meal expenses already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school consolidated the overseas travel expenses of two faculty members in administrative roles for review in the same batch. Prof. Xia, corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel and above (management roles), filed registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs under non-research project funds; the boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were complete and had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; both the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were attached. Prof. Zhou, submitted in the same batch and corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel, had the same listed expense items, receipt preparation, and arrangement of reimbursing all expenses together after return; transport modes included economy-class airfare, hard-seat rail, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin; foreign currency had also been selected in the appointment and converted automatically by the system. However, no original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was found in Prof. Zhou's materials. Neither submission involved overseas book or material purchases, nor any prepayment for future travel.

case_ch: In June 2026, two groups of trainees were arranged in sequence within the same round of themed training, with the administrator submitting both sets of materials together for review. The training for bureau-director-level personnel lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,000, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 350, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,250; an out-of-town instructor's fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 400, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300 were also included; written approval was attached for items exceeding the standard rate, but the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" was not included in this set of materials. The immediately following session for division-level and lower personnel also lasted 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 1,700, meals of RMB 650, venue/materials/transport of RMB 250, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 2,750; the instructor's fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 340, meals of RMB 130, and transport of RMB 300 were likewise included; the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached.

case_ch: In September 2026, the school settled the labor service fees for two experts participating in a series of exchange events in the same batch. Prof. Guo, under the division-level and lower personnel standard, completed a 4-class-hour lecture and participated in a 2-day in-person consultation; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 450 per class hour, with consulting fees at RMB 450 per day. Prof. Cao, a national major research project leader submitted in the same batch, likewise completed a 4-class-hour lecture and a 2-day in-person consultation; lecture fees were also declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 1,200 per class hour, with consulting fees at RMB 1,000 per day. The entire batch of documents covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review and defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the project team submitted the travel reimbursements for two colleagues' Beijing trips to advance the project in the same batch. Prof. Gao, categorized as other personnel, charged expenses to research project funds, traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, filed accommodation under the bureau-director accommodation standard but wrote the actual price as RMB 2,000 per night, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and claimed city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts; the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal, intercity transport invoices, and lodging invoices were all attached. The accompanying Prof. Lu, a national major research project leader, had the same number of travel days, transport mode, and subsidy basis, with an actual lodging rate of RMB 520 per night; however, the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" in Prof. Lu's materials was incomplete. Neither submission included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor of transport or meal subsidies already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school reviewed the overseas conference expenses of two scholars after their return in the same batch. Prof. Qin, corresponding to a national Level-2 professional-technical position personnel, filed registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs under non-research project funds; the boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all attached and had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center. All overseas expenses were reimbursed together after her return, and foreign currency had been selected in the appointment and converted automatically by the system; transport modes included economy-class airfare, hard-seat rail, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin. However, no original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was found in her materials. Accompanying Academician Fan had the same listed expense items, the same transport modes, receipts, and review signatures; both the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were attached. Neither submission involved overseas book or material purchases, nor any prepayment for future travel.

case_ch: In July 2026, the school bound and submitted the materials for two high-level training sessions together for reimbursement. The training session for academician-level participants lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,800; an out-of-town instructor's fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 500, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300 were also included. The other session, for bureau-director-level personnel and above (management roles), also lasted 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880; the instructor fee structure remained the same. The "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached for both sessions, and written approval for items exceeding the standard rate was included for all relevant items.

case_ch: In September 2026, after another college forum concluded, the labor service fees for two experts in management roles were once again processed in the same batch. Prof. Bi, corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel and above (management roles), completed a 4-class-hour lecture and a 2-day in-person consultation; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 1,200 per class hour, with consulting fees at RMB 1,000 per day. Prof. Kong, from the same event and corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel, likewise completed a 4-class-hour lecture and a 2-day in-person consultation, but lecture fees were not declared on an after-tax basis; the lecture rate was RMB 900 per class hour and the in-person consulting rate was RMB 700 per day. The entire batch of documents covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review and defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the project team submitted the travel materials for two colleagues' Beijing trips to carry out a phase of work in the same batch for review. Prof. Meng, bureau-director-level personnel and persons of equivalent rank, charged expenses to research project funds, traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, filed accommodation under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and claimed city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts; however, the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" in his materials was incomplete. The accompanying Prof. Han, a professional-technical personnel at Grade 5 or above on the post-based salary scale, had the same itinerary, transport mode, accommodation standard, and subsidy basis; the "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" and materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal were all fully attached. Both submitted intercity transport invoices and lodging invoices, and neither trip was made by the unit head personally. There were no approved special circumstances explanations or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, no approved special circumstances explanations or handwritten receipts signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained, and no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit or transport and meal subsidies being claimed twice.

case_ch: In 2026, the school conducted a consolidated review of the overseas expenses of two faculty members after their return. Prof. Fang, corresponding to division-level and lower personnel, filed registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs under non-research project funds; the boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were complete and had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were both attached; transport modes included economy-class airfare, hard-seat rail, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin. Prof. Zhang, a national major research project leader, had the same listed expense items and the same pace of reimbursing all expenses together after return; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were likewise complete. Both had selected foreign currency in their appointment booking with conversion handled automatically by the system; neither submission involved overseas book or material purchases or prepayment for future travel.

case_ch: In June 2026, the school organized two more consecutive high-level training sessions, with the reimbursement materials compiled in sequence by the same administrator. The training session for national major research project leaders lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880; an out-of-town instructor's fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 500, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300 were also included; the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached, and written approval for items exceeding the standard rate was included. The other session, for national Level-2 professional-technical position personnel, also lasted 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,800; the instructor fee structure remained the same; however, the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" was not included in this set of materials.

case_ch: In September 2026, the school settled the labor service fees for two high-level experts during an exchange week in the same batch. Prof. Jiang, a national Level-2 professional-technical position personnel, completed a 4-class-hour lecture and participated in a 2-day in-person consultation; lecture fees were not declared on an after-tax basis, with the lecture rate at RMB 1,200 per class hour and the in-person consulting rate at RMB 700 per day. Academician Xi, arriving in the same batch, likewise completed a 4-class-hour lecture and a 2-day in-person consultation; the lecture rate remained at RMB 1,200 per class hour and the consulting rate at RMB 700 per day, but lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis. The entire batch of documents covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review and defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the research team continued to submit the travel materials for two colleagues' Beijing trips to advance a phase of project work together for review. Academician Hou and the accompanying Prof. Song both charged expenses to research project funds, both traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), both stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, both claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300 and city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts. The "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal, intercity transport invoices, and lodging invoices were all attached; neither trip was made by the unit head personally. Academician Hou stayed under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night; Prof. Song, a deputy senior-title and above professional-technical personnel, likewise filed materials under the bureau-director accommodation standard, but the actual lodging rate was written as RMB 2,000 per night. Neither submission included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor any cases of transport or meal expenses already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school reviewed the overseas reimbursements of two faculty members in administrative roles in the same batch. Prof. Wen, corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel and above (management roles), filed registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs under non-research project funds; the boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were complete and had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement were both attached. Prof. Dou, corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel, had the same listed expense items, receipt preparation, and arrangement of reimbursing all expenses together after return; transport modes included economy-class airfare, hard-seat rail, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin; foreign currency had also been selected in the appointment and converted automatically by the system. However, no original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was found in Prof. Dou's materials. Neither submission involved overseas book or material purchases, nor any prepayment for future travel.

case_ch: In June 2026, two additional groups of trainees were arranged within the same round of themed training sessions, and the administrator submitted all reimbursement materials together for review. The training session for bureau-director-level personnel lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,000, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 350, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,250; an out-of-town instructor was also engaged, incurring lecture fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 400, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300; written approval was attached for items exceeding the standard rate, but the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" was not included in this set of materials. The immediately following session for division-level and lower personnel also lasted 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 1,700, meals of RMB 650, venue/materials/transport of RMB 250, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 2,750; the out-of-town instructor's fees remained at RMB 1,000 for lecture fees, RMB 340 for accommodation, RMB 130 for meals, and RMB 300 for transport; for this session, the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached.

case_ch: In September 2026, the school continued to process the labor service fees of two experts who had participated in a series of academic exchange events in the same batch. Prof. Zuo, corresponding to division-level and lower personnel, completed 4 class hours of lecturing and 2 days of in-person consultation; the lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 450 per class hour, and the consultation fee was RMB 450 per day. Prof. Wu, submitted in the same batch and a principal investigator of a major national research project, likewise completed 4 class hours of lecturing and 2 days of in-person consultation; the lecture fees were also declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 1,200 per class hour, and the consultation fee was RMB 1,000 per day. The entire batch of documents covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the research group submitted the travel expenses of two colleagues who had traveled to Beijing for project work in the same batch. Prof. Zou, classified as other personnel, charged expenses to research project funds, traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, filed under the bureau-director accommodation standard but wrote an actual rate of RMB 2,000 per night; a meal subsidy of RMB 300 was claimed, city transport expenses were claimed at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts, and intercity transport invoices, lodging invoices, a "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," and materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal were all organized and included. Prof. Chang, traveling in the same group as a principal investigator of a major national research project, had the same trip duration, transport mode, and subsidy basis, with an actual lodging rate of RMB 520 per night; intercity transport invoices and lodging invoices were also attached, but Prof. Chang's materials did not include a "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form." Neither submission included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor any cases of transport or meal subsidies already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school arranged the overseas expense review for two scholars upon their return in the same batch. Prof. Luo, corresponding to a national-level second-tier professional-technical post, filed under non-research project funds, listing registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs on the overseas expense statement; boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all prepared, and the materials had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; all overseas expenses were reimbursed together after her return, with foreign currency already selected in the appointment and automatically converted by the system; transport modes included economy-class airfare, hard-seat train, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin. However, Prof. Luo's materials did not include the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao." Academician Cheng, submitted in the same batch, had the same listed expense items — registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs — as well as complete receipts and review signatures from the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center; the overseas expense statement was also attached together with the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao," and transport modes likewise included economy-class airfare, hard-seat train, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin. Neither submission involved overseas purchases of books or materials, nor any prepayments for future travel.

case_ch: In July 2026, the school organized the reimbursement materials for two high-level training sessions together for filing. The training session for academician-level participants lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,800; an out-of-town instructor was also engaged, incurring lecture fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 500, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300. The other session was for bureau-director-level and above management personnel, with the same duration of 5 days, accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880, with the same instructor fee structure. For both sessions, the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached, and written approvals were also included for items exceeding the standard rate.

case_ch: In September 2026, the school processed the labor service fees of two management-post experts who had participated in a forum in the same batch. The entire batch of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the research group submitted the travel materials of two teachers who had traveled to Beijing for phase-based project work together for review. Prof. He, classified as bureau-director-level and equivalent-duty personnel, charged expenses to research project funds, traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, filed under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and reported city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts; intercity transport invoices and lodging invoices were organized in the submission, along with materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal. However, no "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" was included in Prof. He's materials. Prof. Bai, a professional-technical personnel with post salary level 5 or above traveling in the same group, had the same trip itinerary, transport mode, accommodation standard, and subsidy basis; Prof. Bai's materials included a complete "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form" as well as documents bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal. Both submissions included intercity transport invoices and lodging invoices, neither trip was made by the unit head personally, and neither submission included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor any cases of transport or meal subsidies already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

Prof. Ruan, a principal investigator of a major national research project, submitted in the same batch, had the same listed expense items and the same arrangement of reimbursing all overseas expenses together after return; the materials also included the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" and the overseas expense statement, and had likewise been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center. Both appointments had foreign currency selected and automatically converted by the system; neither submission involved overseas purchases of books or materials, nor any prepayments for future travel.

case_ch: In June 2026, the school organized two consecutive high-level training sessions, with all reimbursement materials compiled together by the same administrator. The training session for principal investigators of major national research projects lasted 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880; an out-of-town instructor was also engaged, incurring lecture fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 500, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300; the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," the trainee list with contact information, and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider were all attached, and written approval was included for items exceeding the standard rate. The immediately following session was for national-level second-tier professional-technical post personnel, with the same duration of 5 days, accommodation of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,800, with the same instructor fee structure; this set of materials also included the trainee list with contact information and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider, but did not include the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures."

Prof. Mei, a national-level second-tier professional-technical post personnel, both delivered 4 class hours of lecturing and participated in 2 days of in-person consultation; the lecture fees were not declared on an after-tax basis, with a lecturing rate of RMB 1,200 per class hour and an in-person consultation fee of RMB 700 per day. The entire batch of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the research group compiled the travel materials of two teachers who had traveled to Beijing to advance their research project into a single submission for review. Academician Qiao and the accompanying Prof. Tong both charged expenses to research project funds, traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and reported city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts; intercity transport invoices, lodging invoices, and accompanying materials were all organized, and neither trip was made by the unit head personally; both submissions included a "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form." Academician Qiao stayed under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night. Prof. Tong, a deputy senior-title and above professional-technical personnel, filed under the same bureau-director accommodation standard, but the actual lodging rate written on the form was RMB 2,000 per night. Neither submission included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor any cases of transport or meal subsidies already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In May 2026, the research group bound the travel materials of three teachers who had traveled to Beijing for phase-based project tasks together for review. Prof. Cheng, Prof. Xie, and Prof. Dai all charged expenses to research project funds, none of the trips were made by the unit head personally, all traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), each stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, all claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and all reported city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts. Prof. Cheng, an academician, and Prof. Dai, classified as bureau-director-level and equivalent-duty personnel, both stayed under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night. Prof. Xie, a deputy senior-title and above professional-technical personnel, filed under the same standard but the actual lodging rate written on the form was RMB 2,000 per night. The submission included intercity transport invoices, lodging invoices, and city transport receipts; Prof. Cheng and Prof. Xie each attached a "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," while Prof. Dai's form was not included; materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. None of the submissions included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor any cases of transport or meal subsidies already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school combined the overseas expense review for three teachers upon their return in the same batch. Prof. Jiang, Prof. Shao, and Prof. Liang all filed under non-research project funds, listing conference registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs on the overseas expense statement; boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all organized in the same round of materials; all expenses were reimbursed together after return, with foreign currency already selected in each appointment and automatically converted by the system; the entire batch of materials had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center. Prof. Jiang, corresponding to bureau-director-level and above management personnel, did not submit specific transport mode receipts; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was attached. Prof. Shao, corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel, reported transport modes including economy-class airfare, hard-seat train, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin; however, no original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was included. Prof. Liang, corresponding to division-level and lower personnel, also reported transport modes including economy-class airfare, hard-seat train, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin, and the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was also attached. None of the three submissions involved overseas purchases of books or materials, nor any prepayments for future travel.

case_ch: In June 2026, the school organized three consecutive training sessions as part of the same round of capability-enhancement programs. The session managed by Prof. Ding was for bureau-director-level personnel, lasting 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,000, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs totaling RMB 350, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,250; an out-of-town instructor was engaged, incurring lecture fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 400, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300; written approval was included for items exceeding the standard rate, but the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures" was not included for this session. The session managed by Prof. Pan was for division-level and lower personnel, also lasting 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 1,700, meals of RMB 650, venue/materials/transport of RMB 250, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 2,750; the out-of-town instructor's fees were RMB 1,000 for lecture fees, RMB 340 for lodging, RMB 130 for meals, and RMB 300 for transport. The session managed by Prof. Li was for principal investigators of major national research projects, also lasting 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880; the out-of-town instructor's fees were RMB 1,000 for lecture fees, RMB 500 for lodging, RMB 150 for meals, and RMB 300 for transport. The administrator compiled the trainee list with contact information and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider into all materials; the sessions managed by Prof. Pan and Prof. Li also included the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures;" written approvals were attached for all items exceeding the standard rate.

case_ch: In September 2026, the school processed the labor service fees of three experts who had participated in an academic week together in the same batch. Prof. Xu, corresponding to division-level and lower personnel, completed 4 class hours of lecturing and 2 days of in-person consultation; the lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 450 per class hour, and the consultation fee was RMB 450 per day. Prof. Jia, a principal investigator of a major national research project, arrived at the school in the same batch and likewise completed 4 class hours of lecturing and 2 days of in-person consultation; the lecture fees were also declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 1,200 per class hour, and the consultation fee was RMB 1,000 per day. Prof. Yang, a national-level second-tier professional-technical post personnel, also delivered 4 class hours of lecturing and participated in 2 days of in-person consultation, with a lecturing rate of RMB 1,200 per class hour and a consultation fee of RMB 700 per day, but the lecture fees were not declared on an after-tax basis. The entire batch of documents covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the research group submitted the travel expenses of three colleagues who had traveled to Beijing to advance their research project together. Prof. Zhao, Prof. Sun, and Prof. Qian all charged expenses to research project funds, none of the trips were made by the unit head personally, all traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), each stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, all claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and all reported city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts. Prof. Sun, a principal investigator of a major national research project, and Prof. Qian, an academician, both stayed under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night. Prof. Zhao, classified as other personnel, filed under the same standard but the actual lodging rate written on the form was RMB 2,000 per night. The submission included intercity transport invoices, lodging invoices, and city transport receipts; Prof. Zhao and Prof. Qian each attached a "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," while Prof. Sun's was not included; materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. None of the submissions included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor any cases of transport or meal subsidies already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school arranged the overseas expense review for three scholars upon their return in the same batch. Prof. Chen, Prof. Tang, and Prof. Han all filed under non-research project funds, listing conference registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs on the overseas expense statement; boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all organized in the same round of materials; all expenses were reimbursed together after return, with foreign currency already selected in each appointment and automatically converted by the system; the materials had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center. Prof. Chen, a national-level second-tier professional-technical post personnel, reported transport modes including economy-class airfare, hard-seat train, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin; however, no original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was included for Prof. Chen. Prof. Tang, an academician, likewise reported transport modes including economy-class airfare, hard-seat train, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin, and the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was also attached. Prof. Han, corresponding to bureau-director-level and above management personnel, did not submit specific transport mode receipts but also attached the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao." None of the three submissions involved overseas purchases of books or materials, nor any prepayments for future travel.

case_ch: In July 2026, the school continued to compile the materials for three high-level training sessions together for filing. The session managed by Prof. Cheng was for academician-level participants, lasting 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs totaling RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,800; an out-of-town instructor was engaged, incurring lecture fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 500, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300. The session managed by Prof. Xie was for bureau-director-level and above management personnel, also lasting 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880, with the same instructor fee structure. The session managed by Prof. Dai was for bureau-director-level personnel, also lasting 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 2,000, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 350, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,250; the out-of-town instructor's fees were RMB 1,000 for lecture fees, RMB 400 for lodging, RMB 150 for meals, and RMB 300 for transport. The administrator compiled the trainee list with contact information and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider into all materials; the sessions managed by Prof. Cheng and Prof. Xie also included the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," while this document was not included for Prof. Dai's session; written approvals were attached for all items exceeding the standard rate.

case_ch: In September 2026, after a school forum concluded, the labor service fees of three experts from management posts and teaching positions were processed in the same batch. Prof. Zhu, corresponding to bureau-director-level and above management personnel, completed 4 class hours of lecturing and 2 days of in-person consultation; the lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at RMB 1,200 per class hour, and the consultation fee was RMB 1,000 per day. Prof. Huo, appearing at the same event and corresponding to bureau-director-level personnel, also delivered 4 class hours of lecturing and participated in 2 days of in-person consultation, but the lecture fees were not declared on an after-tax basis, with a lecturing rate of RMB 900 per class hour and a consultation rate of RMB 700 per day. Prof. Shen, corresponding to division-level and lower personnel, declared the lecture fees on an after-tax basis, with a lecturing rate of RMB 450 per class hour and a consultation fee of RMB 450 per day. The entire batch of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consulting fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the research group continued to submit the travel materials of three teachers who had traveled to Beijing for phase-based project work together for review. Prof. Ding, Prof. Pan, and Prof. Li all charged expenses to research project funds, none of the trips were made by the unit head personally, all traveled by first-class train seating (round trip), each stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of accommodation, all claimed a meal subsidy of RMB 300, and all reported city transport expenses at actual cost of RMB 180 based on receipts. Prof. Ding, classified as bureau-director-level and equivalent-duty personnel, and Prof. Pan, a professional-technical personnel with post salary level 5 or above, both stayed under the bureau-director accommodation standard at an actual rate of RMB 520 per night. Prof. Li, classified as other personnel, filed under the same standard but the actual lodging rate written on the form was RMB 2,000 per night. The submission included intercity transport invoices, lodging invoices, and city transport receipts; Prof. Pan and Prof. Li each attached a "Fudan University Business Trip Report Form," while Prof. Ding's was not included; materials bearing the unit head's signature and the department (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. None of the submissions included the approved special circumstances explanation or other valid supporting documents required when transport invoices cannot be obtained in remote research areas, nor the approved special circumstances explanation and handwritten receipt signed by the payee required when lodging invoices cannot be obtained. There were no instances of intercity transport or lodging expenses being covered by an external unit, nor any cases of transport or meal subsidies already paid by an off-campus unit being claimed again at the university.

case_ch: In 2026, the school continued to combine the overseas expense review for three teachers upon their return in the same batch. Prof. Zhou, Prof. Lin, and Prof. Wan all filed under non-research project funds, listing conference registration fees, living allowance, incidental expenses, and transportation costs on the overseas expense statement; boarding pass, accommodation receipts, visa fee receipts, and insurance fee receipts were all organized in the same round of materials; all expenses were reimbursed together after return, with foreign currency already selected in each appointment and automatically converted by the system; the materials had been reviewed and signed by the Foreign Affairs Office's Travel Center. Prof. Zhou, corresponding to division-level and lower personnel, reported transport modes including economy-class airfare, hard-seat train, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was attached. Prof. Lin, a principal investigator of a major national research project, did not submit specific transport mode receipts; the original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was likewise attached. Prof. Wan, a national-level second-tier professional-technical post personnel, reported transport modes including economy-class airfare, hard-seat train, second-class seat on high-speed rail or EMU, and third-class ship cabin; however, no original or photocopy of the "Approval Document for Overseas Travel or Travel to Hong Kong and Macao" was included. None of the three submissions involved overseas purchases of books or materials, nor any prepayments for future travel.

case_ch: In June 2026, the school again organized three consecutive high-level training sessions, with all reimbursement materials organized by the same administrator in sequence. The session managed by Prof. Zhao was for principal investigators of major national research projects, lasting 5 days, with accommodation expenses of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport costs totaling RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 230, totaling RMB 3,880; an out-of-town instructor was engaged, incurring lecture fees of RMB 1,000 plus lodging of RMB 500, meals of RMB 150, and transport of RMB 300. The session managed by Prof. Sun was for national-level second-tier professional-technical post personnel, also lasting 5 days, with accommodation of RMB 2,500, meals of RMB 750, venue/materials/transport of RMB 400, and other expenses of RMB 150, totaling RMB 3,800, with the same instructor fee structure. The session managed by Prof. Qian was for academician-level participants, also lasting 5 days, with the same expense breakdown of RMB 2,500 accommodation, RMB 750 meals, RMB 400 venue/materials/transport, and RMB 150 other expenses, totaling RMB 3,800, with the same instructor fee structure. The administrator compiled the trainee list with contact information and the itemized expense receipts provided by the training service provider into all materials; the sessions managed by Prof. Zhao and Prof. Qian also included the "Fudan University Detailed List of Training Expense Expenditures," while this document was not included for Prof. Sun's session; written approvals were attached for all items exceeding the standard rate.
