case_ch: In 2026, the Academic Affairs Office compiled the labor service fees for several academic activities from the 'Thematic Workshop' into a single round of materials. All activities coordinated by Prof. Xu included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Shao included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the academician standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Qiu included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national senior management official at senior deputy-ministerial level or above standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 1,000 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Peng included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the bureau-director-level standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 900 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. The entire round of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense-related expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Intelligent Interdisciplinary Group submitted travel reimbursement documents for all accompanying faculty members together when they traveled to Beijing for project progress communications. Prof. Wu, Prof. Chen, Prof. Tang, and Prof. Han all charged expenses to the research project fund; none was the unit head traveling on personal business. All four traveled by train first class and stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of lodging, each claiming a meal subsidy of 300 yuan and local transportation reimbursed by receipts for 180 yuan. Prof. Wu, Prof. Tang, and Prof. Han were classified as an academician, bureau-level officials (or equivalent duty), and post-salary-scale-5-or-above professional and technical personnel, respectively; all three stayed at the bureau-level lodging quota with actual lodging at 520 yuan per night. Prof. Chen was classified as associate-senior-rank-or-above professional and technical personnel; lodging was filed at the bureau-level standard as well, but the actual price recorded was 2,000 yuan per night. Supporting documents included intercity transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts; Prof. Wu, Prof. Chen, and Prof. Han also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form, and the unit head's signed and department-sealed (primary budget unit) documentation was included. The documents contained no references to transportation ticket certificates for remote research areas, lodging payment receipts, expenses covered by other units, or duplicate claims for transport or meal subsidies already paid by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Digital Humanities Research Group consolidated overseas travel expenses for several faculty members upon their return to China in a single round of materials. Prof. Han, Prof. Yan, Prof. Tao, and Prof. Cheng all reported expenses under the non-research project fund category, with overseas expense statements covering conference registration fees, living allowances, personal incidental expenses, and intercity transportation. All four reimbursed expenses upon returning to China, with international airfare and accommodation paid by bank transfer; foreign currency was selected at the time of appointment and automatically converted by the system. Prof. Han and Prof. Cheng's transport class was submitted at the applicable standard. Prof. Yan and Prof. Tao traveled by economy class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship. Supporting documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, visa fees, and insurance receipts; Prof. Han, Prof. Tao, and Prof. Cheng submitted their documents along with the original or copy of the Approval Document for Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Missions, and the batch was then reviewed and signed by the Office of International Affairs' outbound travel service center. The documents contained no overseas book purchases, overseas material purchases, or prepaid expenses for future travel.

case_ch: In 2026, the Governance Innovation School grouped several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions on 'Interdisciplinary Writing Training' into a single round of training expense documents for joint submission. The session coordinated by Prof. Cheng was primarily for bureau-director-level participants; each session ran for 5 days, with accommodation costs of 2,000 yuan, meal costs of 750 yuan, combined venue/materials/transportation costs of 350 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, totaling 3,250 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with accommodation at 400 yuan, meals at 150 yuan, and intercity transportation at 300 yuan, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Xie was primarily for division-chief-level participants and below; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 1,700 yuan, meals of 650 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 250 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 2,750 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 340 yuan for accommodation, 130 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Dai was primarily for leaders of major national research projects; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 230 yuan, for a total of 3,880 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Zheng was primarily for national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 3,800 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The administrator compiled supporting documents including a trainee roster with contact information and the itemized receipts from the training service provider; the sessions coordinated by Prof. Xie and Prof. Dai also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Medical Humanities Project Team compiled the labor service fees for several academic activities from the 'Research Outcomes Exchange Meeting' into a single round of materials. All activities coordinated by Prof. Yan included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the division-chief-level-and-below standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 450 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 450 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Tao included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the leader-of-major-national-research-project standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 1,000 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Cheng included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Xie included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the academician standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. The entire round of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense-related expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Data Governance Team submitted travel reimbursement documents for all accompanying faculty members together when they traveled to Beijing for phased task wrap-up. Prof. Shao, Prof. Liang, Prof. Cao, and Prof. Song all charged expenses to the research project fund; none was the unit head traveling on personal business. All four traveled by train first class and stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of lodging, each claiming a meal subsidy of 300 yuan and local transportation reimbursed by receipts for 180 yuan. Prof. Liang and Prof. Cao were classified as a leader of a major national research project and an academician, respectively; both stayed at the bureau-level lodging quota with actual lodging at 520 yuan per night. Prof. Shao and Prof. Song were classified as other personnel and associate-senior-rank-or-above professional and technical personnel, respectively; lodging for both was filed at the bureau-level standard as well, but the actual prices recorded were 2,000 yuan per night. Supporting documents included intercity transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts; Prof. Shao, Prof. Cao, and Prof. Song also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form, and the unit head's signed and department-sealed (primary budget unit) documentation was included. The documents contained no references to transportation ticket certificates for remote research areas, lodging payment receipts, expenses covered by other units, or duplicate claims for transport or meal subsidies already paid by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Frontier Brain Science Team consolidated overseas travel expenses for several faculty members upon their return to China in a single round of materials. Prof. Song, Prof. Ding, Prof. Pan, and Prof. Li all reported expenses under the non-research project fund category, with overseas expense statements covering conference registration fees, living allowances, personal incidental expenses, and intercity transportation. All four reimbursed expenses upon returning to China, with international airfare and accommodation paid by bank transfer; foreign currency was selected at the time of appointment and automatically converted by the system. Prof. Pan's transport class was submitted at the applicable standard. Prof. Song, Prof. Ding, and Prof. Li traveled by economy class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship. Supporting documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, visa fees, and insurance receipts; Prof. Ding and Prof. Pan submitted their documents along with the original or copy of the Approval Document for Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Missions, and the batch was then reviewed and signed by the Office of International Affairs' outbound travel service center. The documents contained no overseas book purchases, overseas material purchases, or prepaid expenses for future travel.

case_ch: In 2026, the Interdisciplinary Research Training Camp grouped several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions on 'Research Organization and Collaboration' into a single round of training expense documents for joint submission. The session coordinated by Prof. Li was primarily for academician-level participants; each session ran for 5 days, with accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meal costs of 750 yuan, combined venue/materials/transportation costs of 400 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, totaling 3,800 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with accommodation at 500 yuan, meals at 150 yuan, and intercity transportation at 300 yuan, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Lu was primarily for national senior management officials at senior deputy-ministerial level and above; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 230 yuan, for a total of 3,880 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with accommodation at 500 yuan, meals at 150 yuan, and intercity transportation at 300 yuan, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Peng was primarily for bureau-director-level participants; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,000 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 350 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 3,250 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with accommodation at 400 yuan, meals at 150 yuan, and intercity transportation at 300 yuan, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Zhou was primarily for division-chief-level participants and below; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 1,700 yuan, meals of 650 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 250 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 2,750 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with accommodation at 340 yuan, meals at 130 yuan, and intercity transportation at 300 yuan, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The administrator compiled supporting documents including a trainee roster with contact information and the itemized receipts from the training service provider; the sessions coordinated by Prof. Li, Prof. Lu, and Prof. Zhou also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Social Survey Center compiled the labor service fees for several academic activities from the 'Academic Workshop' into a single round of materials. All activities coordinated by Prof. Zhou included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national senior management official at senior deputy-ministerial level or above standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 1,000 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Lin included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the bureau-director-level standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 900 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Wan included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the division-chief-level-and-below standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 450 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 450 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Shen included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the leader-of-major-national-research-project standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 1,000 yuan per person-day. The entire round of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense-related expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Humanities Computing Laboratory submitted travel reimbursement documents for all accompanying faculty members together when they traveled to Beijing for a themed document review session. Prof. Shen, Prof. Gu, Prof. Zhao, and Prof. Sun all charged expenses to the research project fund; none was the unit head traveling on personal business. All four traveled by train first class and stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of lodging, each claiming a meal subsidy of 300 yuan and local transportation reimbursed by receipts for 180 yuan. Prof. Shen, Prof. Gu, and Prof. Sun were classified as bureau-level officials (or equivalent duty), post-salary-scale-5-or-above professional and technical personnel, and a leader of a major national research project, respectively; all three stayed at the bureau-level lodging quota with actual lodging at 520 yuan per night. Prof. Zhao was classified as other personnel; lodging was filed at the bureau-level standard as well, but the actual price recorded was 2,000 yuan per night. Supporting documents included intercity transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts; Prof. Gu and Prof. Zhao also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form, and the unit head's signed and department-sealed (primary budget unit) documentation was included. The documents contained no references to transportation ticket certificates for remote research areas, lodging payment receipts, expenses covered by other units, or duplicate claims for transport or meal subsidies already paid by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cognitive Science Platform consolidated overseas travel expenses for several faculty members upon their return to China in a single round of materials. Prof. Sun, Prof. Qian, Prof. Feng, and Prof. Wu all reported expenses under the non-research project fund category, with overseas expense statements covering conference registration fees, living allowances, personal incidental expenses, and intercity transportation. All four reimbursed expenses upon returning to China, with international airfare and accommodation paid by bank transfer; foreign currency was selected at the time of appointment and automatically converted by the system. Prof. Qian's transport class was submitted at the applicable standard. Prof. Sun, Prof. Feng, and Prof. Wu traveled by economy class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship. Supporting documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, visa fees, and insurance receipts; Prof. Sun, Prof. Qian, and Prof. Wu submitted their documents along with the original or copy of the Approval Document for Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Missions, and the batch was then reviewed and signed by the Office of International Affairs' outbound travel service center. The documents contained no overseas book purchases, overseas material purchases, or prepaid expenses for future travel.

case_ch: In 2026, the Excellent Teacher Development Center grouped several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions on 'Methodology Training' into a single round of training expense documents for joint submission. The session coordinated by Prof. Wu was primarily for leaders of major national research projects; each session ran for 5 days, with accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meal costs of 750 yuan, combined venue/materials/transportation costs of 400 yuan, and other expenses of 230 yuan, totaling 3,880 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with accommodation at 500 yuan, meals at 150 yuan, and intercity transportation at 300 yuan, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Chen was primarily for national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 3,800 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Tang was primarily for academician-level participants; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 3,800 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Han was primarily for national senior management officials at senior deputy-ministerial level and above; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 230 yuan, for a total of 3,880 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The administrator compiled supporting documents including a trainee roster with contact information and the itemized receipts from the training service provider; the sessions coordinated by Prof. Wu, Prof. Tang, and Prof. Han also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Interdisciplinary Innovation Platform compiled the labor service fees for several academic activities from the 'Interdisciplinary Research Forum' into a single round of materials. All activities coordinated by Prof. Zhao included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Sun included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the academician standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Qian included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national senior management official at senior deputy-ministerial level or above standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 1,000 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Feng included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the bureau-director-level standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 900 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. The entire round of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense-related expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Fundamental Medicine Platform submitted travel reimbursement documents for all accompanying faculty members together when they traveled to Beijing for a joint research synthesis meeting. Prof. Cheng, Prof. Xie, Prof. Dai, and Prof. Zheng all charged expenses to the research project fund; none was the unit head traveling on personal business. All four traveled by train first class and stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of lodging, each claiming a meal subsidy of 300 yuan and local transportation reimbursed by receipts for 180 yuan. Prof. Cheng, Prof. Dai, and Prof. Zheng were classified as an academician, bureau-level officials (or equivalent duty), and post-salary-scale-5-or-above professional and technical personnel, respectively; all three stayed at the bureau-level lodging quota with actual lodging at 520 yuan per night. Prof. Xie was classified as associate-senior-rank-or-above professional and technical personnel; lodging was filed at the bureau-level standard as well, but the actual price recorded was 2,000 yuan per night. Supporting documents included intercity transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts; Prof. Cheng, Prof. Xie, and Prof. Zheng also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form, and the unit head's signed and department-sealed (primary budget unit) documentation was included. The documents contained no references to transportation ticket certificates for remote research areas, lodging payment receipts, expenses covered by other units, or duplicate claims for transport or meal subsidies already paid by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Public Health Research Group consolidated overseas travel expenses for several faculty members upon their return to China in a single round of materials. Prof. Zheng, Prof. Xu, Prof. Jiang, and Prof. Shao all reported expenses under the non-research project fund category, with overseas expense statements covering conference registration fees, living allowances, personal incidental expenses, and intercity transportation. All four reimbursed expenses upon returning to China, with international airfare and accommodation paid by bank transfer; foreign currency was selected at the time of appointment and automatically converted by the system. Prof. Zheng and Prof. Shao's transport class was submitted at the applicable standard. Prof. Xu and Prof. Jiang traveled by economy class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship. Supporting documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, visa fees, and insurance receipts; Prof. Zheng, Prof. Jiang, and Prof. Shao submitted their documents along with the original or copy of the Approval Document for Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Missions, and the batch was then reviewed and signed by the Office of International Affairs' outbound travel service center. The documents contained no overseas book purchases, overseas material purchases, or prepaid expenses for future travel.

case_ch: In 2026, the Youth Scholars Academy grouped several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions on 'Specialized Capacity Building' into a single round of training expense documents for joint submission. The session coordinated by Prof. Shao was primarily for bureau-director-level participants; each session ran for 5 days, with accommodation costs of 2,000 yuan, meal costs of 750 yuan, combined venue/materials/transportation costs of 350 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, totaling 3,250 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with accommodation at 400 yuan, meals at 150 yuan, and intercity transportation at 300 yuan, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Liang was primarily for division-chief-level participants and below; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 1,700 yuan, meals of 650 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 250 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 2,750 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 340 yuan for accommodation, 130 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Cao was primarily for leaders of major national research projects; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 230 yuan, for a total of 3,880 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Song was primarily for national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 3,800 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The administrator compiled supporting documents including a trainee roster with contact information and the itemized receipts from the training service provider; the sessions coordinated by Prof. Liang and Prof. Cao also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Frontier Forum Secretariat compiled the labor service fees for several academic activities from the 'Project Demonstration Forum' into a single round of materials. All activities coordinated by Prof. Wu included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the division-chief-level-and-below standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 450 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 450 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Chen included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the leader-of-major-national-research-project standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 1,000 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Tang included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Han included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the academician standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. The entire round of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense-related expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Public Health Research Group submitted travel reimbursement documents for all accompanying faculty members together when they traveled to Beijing for a centralized data review. Prof. Li, Prof. Lu, Prof. Peng, and Prof. Zhou all charged expenses to the research project fund; none was the unit head traveling on personal business. All four traveled by train first class and stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of lodging, each claiming a meal subsidy of 300 yuan and local transportation reimbursed by receipts for 180 yuan. Prof. Lu and Prof. Peng were classified as a leader of a major national research project and an academician, respectively; both stayed at the bureau-level lodging quota with actual lodging at 520 yuan per night. Prof. Li and Prof. Zhou were classified as other personnel and associate-senior-rank-or-above professional and technical personnel, respectively; lodging for both was filed at the bureau-level standard as well, but the actual prices recorded were 2,000 yuan per night. Supporting documents included intercity transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts; Prof. Li, Prof. Peng, and Prof. Zhou also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form, and the unit head's signed and department-sealed (primary budget unit) documentation was included. The documents contained no references to transportation ticket certificates for remote research areas, lodging payment receipts, expenses covered by other units, or duplicate claims for transport or meal subsidies already paid by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the International Cooperation Office consolidated overseas travel expenses for several faculty members upon their return to China in a single round of materials. Prof. Zhou, Prof. Lin, Prof. Wan, and Prof. Shen all reported expenses under the non-research project fund category, with overseas expense statements covering conference registration fees, living allowances, personal incidental expenses, and intercity transportation. All four reimbursed expenses upon returning to China, with international airfare and accommodation paid by bank transfer; foreign currency was selected at the time of appointment and automatically converted by the system. Prof. Wan's transport class was submitted at the applicable standard. Prof. Zhou, Prof. Lin, and Prof. Shen traveled by economy class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship. Supporting documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, visa fees, and insurance receipts; Prof. Lin and Prof. Wan submitted their documents along with the original or copy of the Approval Document for Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Missions, and the batch was then reviewed and signed by the Office of International Affairs' outbound travel service center. The documents contained no overseas book purchases, overseas material purchases, or prepaid expenses for future travel.

case_ch: In 2026, the Medical Data Workshop grouped several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions on 'Project Management Practice' into a single round of training expense documents for joint submission. The session coordinated by Prof. Shen was primarily for academician-level participants; each session ran for 5 days, with accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meal costs of 750 yuan, combined venue/materials/transportation costs of 400 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, totaling 3,800 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with accommodation at 500 yuan, meals at 150 yuan, and intercity transportation at 300 yuan, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Gu was primarily for national senior management officials at senior deputy-ministerial level and above; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 230 yuan, for a total of 3,880 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Zhao was primarily for bureau-director-level participants; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,000 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 350 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 3,250 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 400 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Sun was primarily for division-chief-level participants and below; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 1,700 yuan, meals of 650 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 250 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 2,750 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 340 yuan for accommodation, 130 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The administrator compiled supporting documents including a trainee roster with contact information and the itemized receipts from the training service provider; the sessions coordinated by Prof. Shen, Prof. Gu, and Prof. Sun also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Academic Affairs Office compiled the labor service fees for several academic activities from the 'Thematic Workshop' into a single round of materials. All activities coordinated by Prof. Xu included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national senior management official at senior deputy-ministerial level or above standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 1,000 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Shao included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the bureau-director-level standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 900 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Qiu included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the division-chief-level-and-below standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 450 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 450 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Peng included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the leader-of-major-national-research-project standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 1,000 yuan per person-day. The entire round of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense-related expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Intelligent Interdisciplinary Group submitted travel reimbursement documents for all accompanying faculty members together when they traveled to Beijing for project progress communications. Prof. Wu, Prof. Chen, Prof. Tang, and Prof. Han all charged expenses to the research project fund; none was the unit head traveling on personal business. All four traveled by train first class and stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of lodging, each claiming a meal subsidy of 300 yuan and local transportation reimbursed by receipts for 180 yuan. Prof. Wu, Prof. Chen, and Prof. Han were classified as bureau-level officials (or equivalent duty), post-salary-scale-5-or-above professional and technical personnel, and a leader of a major national research project, respectively; all three stayed at the bureau-level lodging quota with actual lodging at 520 yuan per night. Prof. Tang was classified as other personnel; lodging was filed at the bureau-level standard as well, but the actual price recorded was 2,000 yuan per night. Supporting documents included intercity transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts; Prof. Chen and Prof. Tang also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form, and the unit head's signed and department-sealed (primary budget unit) documentation was included. The documents contained no references to transportation ticket certificates for remote research areas, lodging payment receipts, expenses covered by other units, or duplicate claims for transport or meal subsidies already paid by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Digital Humanities Research Group consolidated overseas travel expenses for several faculty members upon their return to China in a single round of materials. Prof. Han, Prof. Yan, Prof. Tao, and Prof. Cheng all reported expenses under the non-research project fund category, with overseas expense statements covering conference registration fees, living allowances, personal incidental expenses, and intercity transportation. All four reimbursed expenses upon returning to China, with international airfare and accommodation paid by bank transfer; foreign currency was selected at the time of appointment and automatically converted by the system. Prof. Yan's transport class was submitted at the applicable standard. Prof. Han, Prof. Tao, and Prof. Cheng traveled by economy class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship. Supporting documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, visa fees, and insurance receipts; Prof. Han, Prof. Yan, and Prof. Cheng submitted their documents along with the original or copy of the Approval Document for Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Missions, and the batch was then reviewed and signed by the Office of International Affairs' outbound travel service center. The documents contained no overseas book purchases, overseas material purchases, or prepaid expenses for future travel.

case_ch: In 2026, the Governance Innovation School grouped several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions on 'Interdisciplinary Writing Training' into a single round of training expense documents for joint submission. The session coordinated by Prof. Cheng was primarily for leaders of major national research projects; each session ran for 5 days, with accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meal costs of 750 yuan, combined venue/materials/transportation costs of 400 yuan, and other expenses of 230 yuan, totaling 3,880 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with accommodation at 500 yuan, meals at 150 yuan, and intercity transportation at 300 yuan, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Xie was primarily for national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 3,800 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Dai was primarily for academician-level participants; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 3,800 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Zheng was primarily for national senior management officials at senior deputy-ministerial level and above; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 230 yuan, for a total of 3,880 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The administrator compiled supporting documents including a trainee roster with contact information and the itemized receipts from the training service provider; the sessions coordinated by Prof. Cheng, Prof. Dai, and Prof. Zheng also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Medical Humanities Project Team compiled the labor service fees for several academic activities from the 'Research Outcomes Exchange Meeting' into a single round of materials. All activities coordinated by Prof. Yan included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Tao included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the academician standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Cheng included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national senior management official at senior deputy-ministerial level or above standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 1,000 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Xie included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the bureau-director-level standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 900 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. The entire round of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense-related expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Data Governance Team submitted travel reimbursement documents for all accompanying faculty members together when they traveled to Beijing for phased task wrap-up. Prof. Shao, Prof. Liang, Prof. Cao, and Prof. Song all charged expenses to the research project fund; none was the unit head traveling on personal business. All four traveled by train first class and stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights of lodging, each claiming a meal subsidy of 300 yuan and local transportation reimbursed by receipts for 180 yuan. Prof. Shao, Prof. Cao, and Prof. Song were classified as an academician, bureau-level officials (or equivalent duty), and post-salary-scale-5-or-above professional and technical personnel, respectively; all three stayed at the bureau-level lodging quota with actual lodging at 520 yuan per night. Prof. Liang was classified as associate-senior-rank-or-above professional and technical personnel; lodging was filed at the bureau-level standard as well, but the actual price recorded was 2,000 yuan per night. Supporting documents included intercity transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts; Prof. Shao, Prof. Liang, and Prof. Song also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form, and the unit head's signed and department-sealed (primary budget unit) documentation was included. The documents contained no references to transportation ticket certificates for remote research areas, lodging payment receipts, expenses covered by other units, or duplicate claims for transport or meal subsidies already paid by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Frontier Brain Science Team consolidated overseas travel expenses for several faculty members upon their return to China in a single round of materials. Prof. Song, Prof. Ding, Prof. Pan, and Prof. Li all reported expenses under the non-research project fund category, with overseas expense statements covering conference registration fees, living allowances, personal incidental expenses, and intercity transportation. All four reimbursed expenses upon returning to China, with international airfare and accommodation paid by bank transfer; foreign currency was selected at the time of appointment and automatically converted by the system. Prof. Pan's transport class was submitted at the applicable standard. Prof. Song, Prof. Ding, and Prof. Li traveled by economy class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship. Supporting documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, visa fees, and insurance receipts; Prof. Ding and Prof. Pan submitted their documents along with the original or copy of the Approval Document for Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Missions, and the batch was then reviewed and signed by the Office of International Affairs' outbound travel service center. The documents contained no overseas book purchases, overseas material purchases, or prepaid expenses for future travel.

case_ch: In 2026, the Interdisciplinary Research Training Camp grouped several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions on 'Research Organization and Collaboration' into a single round of training expense documents for joint submission. The session coordinated by Prof. Li was primarily for bureau-director-level participants; each session ran for 5 days, with accommodation costs of 2,000 yuan, meal costs of 750 yuan, combined venue/materials/transportation costs of 350 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, totaling 3,250 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with accommodation at 400 yuan, meals at 150 yuan, and intercity transportation at 300 yuan, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Lu was primarily for division-chief-level participants and below; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 1,700 yuan, meals of 650 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 250 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 2,750 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 340 yuan for accommodation, 130 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Peng was primarily for leaders of major national research projects; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 230 yuan, for a total of 3,880 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The session coordinated by Prof. Zhou was primarily for national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel; the 5-day session had accommodation costs of 2,500 yuan, meals of 750 yuan, venue/materials/transport totaling 400 yuan, and other expenses of 150 yuan, for a total of 3,800 yuan; the out-of-town instructor's lecture fee was 1,000 yuan, with 500 yuan for accommodation, 150 yuan for meals, and 300 yuan for intercity transportation, with written approval for exceeding the standard attached. The administrator compiled supporting documents including a trainee roster with contact information and the itemized receipts from the training service provider; the sessions coordinated by Prof. Lu and Prof. Peng also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Social Survey Center compiled the labor service fees for several academic activities from the 'Academic Workshop' into a single round of materials. All activities coordinated by Prof. Zhou included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the division-chief-level-and-below standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 450 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 450 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Lin included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the leader-of-major-national-research-project standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 1,000 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Wan included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the national-level second-tier professional and technical personnel standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on a non-after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. All activities coordinated by Prof. Shen included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; the speaker was classified under the academician standard, with 4 class hours equivalent to 1 half-day, lecture fees declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 yuan per class hour; consultation participants were classified under the same standard, with 2 consecutive days of on-site consultation at 700 yuan per person-day. The entire round of materials covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no graduate thesis review or defense-related expenses.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Humanities Computing Lab traveled to Beijing to conduct a thematic document review session, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all participating faculty together. Teachers Shen, Gu, Zhao, and Sun all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Gu and Zhao, corresponding to Major National Research Project Leader and Academician respectively, both applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Shen and Sun, corresponding to Other Personnel and Deputy-Senior-level or Above Professional-Technical Personnel respectively, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Shen, Zhao, and Sun also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cognitive Science Platform consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Sun, Qian, Feng, and Wu all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Feng's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Sun, Qian, and Wu recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Qian and Feng also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Excellent Teacher Development Center compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Methods Training' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. The session coordinated by Teacher Wu primarily targeted participants at the academician level, running for 5 days, with accommodation expenses of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation costs of 400 RMB combined, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; the instructor came from out of town, with lecture fees of 1,000 RMB plus accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Chen primarily targeted participants at the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel level, running for 5 days, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; the instructor came from out of town, with lecture fees of 1,000 RMB plus accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Tang primarily targeted participants at the bureau-director level, running for 5 days, with accommodation of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor came from out of town, with lecture fees of 1,000 RMB plus accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Han primarily targeted participants at the division-chief level and below, running for 5 days, with accommodation of 1,700 RMB, meals of 650 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 250 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 2,750 RMB; the instructor came from out of town, with lecture fees of 1,000 RMB plus accommodation of 340 RMB, meals of 130 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Wu, Chen, and Han also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cross-Innovation Platform, while advancing the 'Cross-Disciplinary Research Forum,' consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Zhao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Sun all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 900 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Qian all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Feng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Basic Medicine Platform traveled to Beijing to advance a joint survey consolidation task, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all accompanying faculty together. Teachers Cheng, Xie, Dai, and Zheng all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Cheng, Xie, and Zheng, corresponding to bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel, and Major National Research Project Leader respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Dai, corresponding to Other Personnel, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Xie and Dai also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Public Health Research Group consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Zheng, Xu, Jiang, and Shao all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Xu's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Zheng, Jiang, and Shao recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Zheng, Xu, and Shao also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Young Scholars Academy compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Thematic Capability Enhancement' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. The session coordinated by Teacher Shao primarily targeted participants who are Major National Research Project Leaders, running for 5 days, with accommodation expenses of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation costs of 400 RMB combined, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; the instructor came from out of town, with lecture fees of 1,000 RMB plus accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Liang primarily targeted participants at the national professional-technical post level 2, running for 5 days, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; the instructor came from out of town, with lecture fees of 1,000 RMB plus accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Cao primarily targeted participants at the academician level, running for 5 days, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; the instructor came from out of town, with lecture fees of 1,000 RMB plus accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Song primarily targeted participants at the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel level, running for 5 days, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; the instructor came from out of town, with lecture fees of 1,000 RMB plus accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Shao, Cao, and Song also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Frontier Forum Secretariat, while advancing the 'Project Demonstration Meeting,' consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Wu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Chen all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the academician-level standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the academician-level standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Tang all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Han all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 900 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Public Health Research Group traveled to Beijing to advance a document consolidation and review task, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all accompanying faculty together. Teachers Li, Lu, Peng, and Zhou all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Li, Peng, and Zhou, corresponding to Academician, bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, and post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Lu, corresponding to deputy-senior-level or above professional-technical personnel, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Li, Lu, and Zhou also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the International Cooperation Office consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Zhou, Lin, Wan, and Shen all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Zhou and Shen's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Lin and Wan recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Zhou, Wan, and Shen also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Humanities Computing Lab traveled to Beijing to conduct a thematic document review session, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Shen, Gu, Zhao, Sun, and Qian all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Shen, Zhao, and Sun, corresponding to Academician, bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, and post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Gu and Qian, corresponding to deputy-senior-level or above professional-technical personnel and Other Personnel respectively, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Shen, Gu, Sun, and Qian also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cognitive Science Platform consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Sun, Qian, Feng, Wu, and Chen all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Sun and Wu's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Qian, Feng, and Chen recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Sun, Feng, and Wu also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.
