case_ch: In 2026, the Excellent Teacher Development Center compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Methods Training' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Wu, Chen, Tang, Han, and Yan ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Wu targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation expenses of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Chen targeted division-chief-level and below participants, with accommodation of 1,700 RMB, meals of 650 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 250 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 2,750 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 340 RMB, meals of 130 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Tang targeted Major National Research Project Leaders, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Han and Yan targeted national professional-technical post level 2 personnel and academician-level participants respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Chen, Tang, and Yan also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cross-Innovation Platform, while advancing the 'Cross-Disciplinary Research Forum,' consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Xu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Shao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Qiu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Peng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the academician-level standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the academician-level standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Cao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Basic Medicine Platform traveled to Beijing to advance a joint survey consolidation task, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Cheng, Xie, Dai, Zheng, and Xu all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Xie, Dai, and Xu, corresponding to Major National Research Project Leader, Academician, and bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Cheng and Zheng, corresponding to Other Personnel and deputy-senior-level or above professional-technical personnel respectively, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Cheng, Dai, and Zheng also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Public Health Research Group consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Zheng, Xu, Jiang, Shao, and Liang all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Jiang's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Zheng, Xu, Shao, and Liang recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Xu, Jiang, and Liang also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Young Scholars Academy compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Thematic Capability Enhancement' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Shao, Liang, Cao, Song, and Ding ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Shao targeted academician-level participants, with accommodation expenses of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Liang and Ding targeted national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel and Major National Research Project Leaders respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Cao targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Song targeted division-chief-level and below participants, with accommodation of 1,700 RMB, meals of 650 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 250 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 2,750 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 340 RMB, meals of 130 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Shao, Liang, Song, and Ding also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Frontier Forum Secretariat, while advancing the 'Project Demonstration Meeting,' consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Yan all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Tao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 900 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Cheng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Xie all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Dai all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Public Health Research Group traveled to Beijing to advance a document consolidation and review task, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Li, Lu, Peng, Zhou, and Lin all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Li, Lu, Zhou, and Lin, corresponding to bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel, Major National Research Project Leader, and Academician respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Peng, corresponding to Other Personnel, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Lu, Peng, and Lin also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the International Cooperation Office consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Zhou, Lin, Wan, Shen, and Gu all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Lin and Gu's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Zhou, Wan, and Shen recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Zhou, Lin, Shen, and Gu also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Medical Data Workshop compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Project Management Practice' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Shen, Gu, Zhao, Sun, and Qian ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Shen and Sun targeted Major National Research Project Leaders and national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel respectively, with accommodation expenses of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Gu and Zhao targeted national professional-technical post level 2 personnel and academician-level participants respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Qian targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Shen, Zhao, and Sun also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Academic Affairs Office, while advancing the 'Thematic Workshop' series, consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Zhou all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Lin all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the academician-level standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the academician-level standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Wan all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Shen all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 900 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Gu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the AI Cross-Disciplinary Group traveled to Beijing to advance a project progress communication meeting, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Wu, Chen, Tang, Han, and Yan all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Wu, Tang, and Han, corresponding to Academician, bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, and post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Chen and Yan, corresponding to deputy-senior-level or above professional-technical personnel and Other Personnel respectively, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Wu, Chen, Han, and Yan also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Digital Humanities Research Group consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Han, Yan, Tao, Cheng, and Xie all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Han and Cheng's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Yan, Tao, and Xie recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Han, Tao, and Cheng also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Governance Innovation Institute compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Cross-Disciplinary Writing Training' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Cheng, Xie, Dai, Zheng, and Xu ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Cheng targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation expenses of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Xie targeted division-chief-level and below participants, with accommodation of 1,700 RMB, meals of 650 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 250 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 2,750 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 340 RMB, meals of 130 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Dai targeted Major National Research Project Leaders, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Zheng and Xu targeted national professional-technical post level 2 personnel and academician-level participants respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Xie, Dai, and Xu also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Medical Humanities Project Team, while advancing the 'Results Exchange Meeting,' consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Zhao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Sun all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Qian all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Feng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the academician-level standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the academician-level standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Zheng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Data Governance Team traveled to Beijing to finalize a phased task wrap-up, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Shao, Liang, Cao, Song, and Ding all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Liang, Cao, and Ding, corresponding to Major National Research Project Leader, Academician, and bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Shao and Song, corresponding to Other Personnel and deputy-senior-level or above professional-technical personnel respectively, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Shao, Cao, and Song also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Frontier Brain Science Team consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Song, Ding, Pan, Li, and Lu all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Pan's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Song, Ding, Li, and Lu recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Ding, Pan, and Lu also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cross-Disciplinary Research Training Camp compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Research Organization and Collaboration' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Li, Lu, Peng, Zhou, and Lin ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Li targeted academician-level participants, with accommodation expenses of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Lu and Lin targeted national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel and Major National Research Project Leaders respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Peng targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Zhou targeted division-chief-level and below participants, with accommodation of 1,700 RMB, meals of 650 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 250 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 2,750 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 340 RMB, meals of 130 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Li, Lu, Zhou, and Lin also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Social Survey Center, while advancing the 'Academic Workshop' series, consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Wu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Chen all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 900 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Tang all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Han all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Jiang all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Humanities Computing Lab traveled to Beijing to conduct a thematic document review session, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Shen, Gu, Zhao, Sun, and Qian all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Shen, Gu, Sun, and Qian, corresponding to bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel, Major National Research Project Leader, and Academician respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Zhao, corresponding to Other Personnel, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Gu, Zhao, and Qian also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cognitive Science Platform consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Sun, Qian, Feng, Wu, and Chen all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Qian and Chen's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Sun, Feng, and Wu recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Sun, Qian, Wu, and Chen also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Excellent Teacher Development Center compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Methods Training' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Wu, Chen, Tang, Han, and Yan ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Wu and Han targeted Major National Research Project Leaders and national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel respectively, with accommodation expenses of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Chen and Tang targeted national professional-technical post level 2 personnel and academician-level participants respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Yan targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Wu, Tang, and Han also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cross-Innovation Platform, while advancing the 'Cross-Disciplinary Research Forum,' consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Xu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Shao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the academician-level standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the academician-level standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Qiu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Peng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 900 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Cao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Basic Medicine Platform traveled to Beijing to advance a joint survey consolidation task, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Cheng, Xie, Dai, Zheng, and Xu all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Cheng, Dai, and Zheng, corresponding to Academician, bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, and post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Xie and Xu, corresponding to deputy-senior-level or above professional-technical personnel and Other Personnel respectively, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Cheng, Xie, Zheng, and Xu also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Public Health Research Group consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Zheng, Xu, Jiang, Shao, and Liang all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Zheng and Shao's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Xu, Jiang, and Liang recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Zheng, Jiang, and Shao also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Young Scholars Academy compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Thematic Capability Enhancement' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Shao, Liang, Cao, Song, and Ding ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Shao targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation expenses of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Liang targeted division-chief-level and below participants, with accommodation of 1,700 RMB, meals of 650 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 250 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 2,750 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 340 RMB, meals of 130 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Cao targeted Major National Research Project Leaders, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Song and Ding targeted national professional-technical post level 2 personnel and academician-level participants respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Liang, Cao, and Ding also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Frontier Forum Secretariat, while advancing the 'Project Demonstration Meeting,' consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Yan all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Tao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Cheng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Xie all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the academician-level standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the academician-level standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Dai all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Public Health Research Group traveled to Beijing to advance a document consolidation and review task, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Li, Lu, Peng, Zhou, and Lin all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Lu, Peng, and Lin, corresponding to Major National Research Project Leader, Academician, and bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Li and Zhou, corresponding to Other Personnel and deputy-senior-level or above professional-technical personnel respectively, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Li, Peng, and Zhou also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the International Cooperation Office consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Zhou, Lin, Wan, Shen, and Gu all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Wan's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Zhou, Lin, Shen, and Gu recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Lin, Wan, and Gu also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Medical Data Workshop compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Project Management Practice' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Shen, Gu, Zhao, Sun, and Qian ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Shen targeted academician-level participants, with accommodation expenses of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Gu and Qian targeted national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel and Major National Research Project Leaders respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Zhao targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Sun targeted division-chief-level and below participants, with accommodation of 1,700 RMB, meals of 650 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 250 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 2,750 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 340 RMB, meals of 130 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Shen, Gu, Sun, and Qian also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Academic Affairs Office, while advancing the 'Thematic Workshop' series, consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Zhou all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Lin all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 900 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Wan all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Shen all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Gu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the AI Cross-Disciplinary Group traveled to Beijing to advance a project progress communication meeting, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Wu, Chen, Tang, Han, and Yan all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Wu, Chen, Han, and Yan, corresponding to bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel, Major National Research Project Leader, and Academician respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Tang, corresponding to Other Personnel, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Chen, Tang, and Yan also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Digital Humanities Research Group consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Han, Yan, Tao, Cheng, and Xie all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Yan and Xie's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Han, Tao, and Cheng recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Han, Yan, Cheng, and Xie also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Governance Innovation Institute compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Cross-Disciplinary Writing Training' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Cheng, Xie, Dai, Zheng, and Xu ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Cheng and Zheng targeted Major National Research Project Leaders and national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel respectively, with accommodation expenses of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Xie and Dai targeted national professional-technical post level 2 personnel and academician-level participants respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Xu targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Cheng, Dai, and Zheng also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Medical Humanities Project Team, while advancing the 'Results Exchange Meeting,' consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Zhao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Sun all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the academician-level standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the academician-level standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Qian all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Feng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 900 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Zheng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Data Governance Team traveled to Beijing to finalize a phased task wrap-up, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Shao, Liang, Cao, Song, and Ding all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Shao, Cao, and Song, corresponding to Academician, bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, and post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Liang and Ding, corresponding to deputy-senior-level or above professional-technical personnel and Other Personnel respectively, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Shao, Liang, Song, and Ding also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Frontier Brain Science Team consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Song, Ding, Pan, Li, and Lu all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Song and Li's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Ding, Pan, and Lu recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Song, Pan, and Li also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cross-Disciplinary Research Training Camp compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Research Organization and Collaboration' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Li, Lu, Peng, Zhou, and Lin ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Li targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation expenses of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Lu targeted division-chief-level and below participants, with accommodation of 1,700 RMB, meals of 650 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 250 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 2,750 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 340 RMB, meals of 130 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Peng targeted Major National Research Project Leaders, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Zhou and Lin targeted national professional-technical post level 2 personnel and academician-level participants respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Lu, Peng, and Lin also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Social Survey Center, while advancing the 'Academic Workshop' series, consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Wu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Chen all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Tang all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Han all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the academician-level standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the academician-level standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Jiang all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.
