case_ch: In May 2026, the Humanities Computing Lab traveled to Beijing to conduct a thematic document review session, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Shen, Gu, Zhao, Sun, and Qian all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Gu, Zhao, and Qian, corresponding to Major National Research Project Leader, Academician, and bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Shen and Sun, corresponding to Other Personnel and deputy-senior-level or above professional-technical personnel respectively, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Shen, Zhao, and Sun also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cognitive Science Platform consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Sun, Qian, Feng, Wu, and Chen all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Feng's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Sun, Qian, Wu, and Chen recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Qian, Feng, and Chen also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Excellent Teacher Development Center compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Methods Training' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Wu, Chen, Tang, Han, and Yan ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The session coordinated by Teacher Wu targeted academician-level participants, with accommodation expenses of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Chen and Yan targeted national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel and Major National Research Project Leaders respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Tang targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Han targeted division-chief-level and below participants, with accommodation of 1,700 RMB, meals of 650 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 250 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 2,750 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 340 RMB, meals of 130 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Wu, Chen, Han, and Yan also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Cross-Innovation Platform, while advancing the 'Cross-Disciplinary Research Forum,' consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Xu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Shao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 900 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Qiu all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Peng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the Major National Research Project Leader standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Cao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Basic Medicine Platform traveled to Beijing to advance a joint survey consolidation task, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Cheng, Xie, Dai, Zheng, and Xu all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Cheng, Xie, Zheng, and Xu, corresponding to bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel, Major National Research Project Leader, and Academician respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Dai, corresponding to Other Personnel, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Xie, Dai, and Xu also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the Public Health Research Group consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Zheng, Xu, Jiang, Shao, and Liang all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Xu and Liang's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Zheng, Jiang, and Shao recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Zheng, Xu, Shao, and Liang also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.

case_ch: In 2026, the Young Scholars Academy compiled several consecutively scheduled thematic training sessions under the theme of 'Thematic Capability Enhancement' into a single training expense reimbursement batch. All sessions coordinated by Teachers Shao, Liang, Cao, Song, and Ding ran for 5 days, with out-of-town instructors engaged for each session at a lecture fee of 1,000 RMB, along with written approval for the elevated standard. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Shao and Song targeted Major National Research Project Leaders and national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel respectively, with accommodation expenses of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 230 RMB, totaling 3,880 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The sessions coordinated by Teachers Liang and Cao targeted national professional-technical post level 2 personnel and academician-level participants respectively, with accommodation of 2,500 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 400 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,800 RMB; each instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 500 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The session coordinated by Teacher Ding targeted bureau-director-level participants, with accommodation of 2,000 RMB, meals of 750 RMB, venue/materials/transportation of 350 RMB, and other expenses of 150 RMB, totaling 3,250 RMB; the instructor's additional expenses included accommodation of 400 RMB, meals of 150 RMB, and inter-city transportation of 300 RMB. The coordinator compiled the trainee roster with contact information and itemized receipts from the training service provider into the submission materials; the sessions coordinated by Teachers Shao, Cao, and Song also included a Fudan University Training Expense Detailed List.

case_ch: In 2026, the Frontier Forum Secretariat, while advancing the 'Project Demonstration Meeting,' consolidated labor service fees from several academic activities into a single review batch. The activities coordinated by Teacher Yan all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national professional-technical post level 2 standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Tao all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the academician-level standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the academician-level standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Cheng all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 1,200 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the national ministry/bureau-level or above management personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 1,000 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Xie all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on a pre-tax (non-after-tax) basis at 900 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the bureau-director-level personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 700 RMB per person per day. The activities coordinated by Teacher Dai all included both lecture fees and expert consultation fees; lecturers were classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with 4 class hours equaling 1 half-day; lecture fees were declared on an after-tax basis at 450 RMB per class hour; consultation participants were also classified under the division-chief-level and below personnel standard, with in-person consultation sessions spanning 2 consecutive days at 450 RMB per person per day. The entire batch covered only lecture fees and expert consultation fees, with no mention of graduate thesis review, dissertation defense fees, or related items.

case_ch: In May 2026, the Public Health Research Group traveled to Beijing to advance a document consolidation and review task, submitting travel reimbursement requests for all five accompanying faculty together. Teachers Li, Lu, Peng, Zhou, and Lin all charged their expenses to research project funds, and none was the unit head traveling on official business; all reported round-trip travel by first-class train, stayed in Beijing for 3 days with 2 nights' lodging, claimed a meal subsidy of 300 RMB each, and reported 180 RMB in local transportation per actual receipts. Li, Peng, and Zhou, corresponding to Academician, bureau-or-equivalent-duty personnel, and post-salary-level-5-or-above professional-technical personnel respectively, all applied the bureau-level lodging standard, with actual nightly rates of 520 RMB. Lu and Lin, corresponding to deputy-senior-level or above professional-technical personnel and Other Personnel respectively, also submitted lodging claims at the bureau-level standard, but the actual nightly rate recorded was 2,000 RMB. The supporting documents included inter-city transportation invoices, lodging invoices, and local transportation receipts. Li, Lu, Zhou, and Lin also attached a Fudan University Business Trip Report Form; the unit head's signature and the department's (first-level budget unit) official seal were also included. The entire batch contained no references to remote-area transportation certificates, lodging payment vouchers, costs shared by other units, or double-claiming of transportation or meal subsidies already covered by external organizations.

case_ch: In 2026, the International Cooperation Office consolidated the overseas travel expense reimbursements for several faculty members returning from abroad into a single review batch. Teachers Zhou, Lin, Wan, Shen, and Gu all charged their expenses to non-research project funds; all overseas expense statements listed conference registration fees, overseas living allowance, personal incidental expenses, and inter-city transportation costs, and all reimbursements were submitted collectively after returning to China. International airfare and accommodation were paid via bank transfer, with foreign currency selected at time of booking and automatically converted by the system. Zhou and Shen's transportation class was reported in accordance with the applicable standard, while Lin, Wan, and Gu recorded their travel mode as economy-class air, hard-seat train, second-class high-speed rail, and third-class ship respectively. The submitted documents included overseas expense statements, airfare boarding passes, accommodation receipts, and visa and insurance fee vouchers. Zhou, Wan, and Shen also submitted the original or photocopy of the Overseas and Hong Kong/Macao Mission Approval Form, which was then forwarded to the Foreign Affairs Office Overseas Travel Service Center for review and signature. The materials contained no entries for overseas book purchases, overseas materials purchases, or advance payments for subsequent itineraries.
